faq.a1: Onam is celebrated during the Malayalam month of Chingam, usually in August or September.
faq.a2: Onam celebrates the annual homecoming of King Mahabali and honors Lord Vishnu’s Vamana Avatar.
faq.a3: Onam is primarily celebrated in Kerala and by Malayali communities around the world.
faq.q4: How many days does Onam last?
faq.a4: Onam is celebrated as a ten-day festival.
faq.a5: Pookalam is a colorful floral decoration made with fresh flowers to welcome King Mahabali and celebrate Onam.
faq.a6: Onam Sadya is a traditional multi-course vegetarian feast served on a banana leaf during the festival.
faq.q7: Which deity is worshipped during Onam?
faq.a7: Lord Vamana, the fifth incarnation of Lord Vishnu, is worshipped during Onam.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Onam?
faq.a8: Onam symbolizes prosperity, gratitude, humility, righteousness, family unity, cultural heritage, and the blessings of Lord Vishnu.
